proposals.title: C29 freq +1: int64_t cents fix ignored — Kahan used instead
proposals.detect: Q3 all 6 iters: mismatch_pattern=precision_loss, magnitude ~10^13, abs_diff 0.01–0.05. storage_design.json had explicit C29 WARNING on value column prescribing int64_t cents. Code generator applied Kahan-Neumaier long double instead. C29 states Kahan does NOT fix this.
proposals.fix: When storage_design.json notes say 'C29 WARNING: SUM must accumulate as int64_t cents: iv=llround(v*100); sum+=iv', that annotation MUST be followed literally. The code inspector must reject any KahanSum struct or compensated double accumulation in C29-applicable contexts.

proposals.title: NEW C30: Exact N× factor mismatch across ALL aggregate columns = missing GROUP BY key dimension
proposals.detect: Validation shows rows where expected_value / actual_value = exact integer N (e.g. 2.0, 3.0) AND expected_count / actual_count = same N simultaneously. This is the diagnostic signature of a missing GROUP BY key in the hash map: N groups with the same partial key are merged into one, producing N× the correct sum and count.
proposals.fix: When 50% (or exact N×) mismatch appears in both value and count simultaneously, audit the hash map key against the full GROUP BY clause. Add ALL missing dimensions to the key. Example: Q6 iter_2 grouped by (name, tag, plabel) but map key was missing one dimension — BUENAVENTURA had expected 210 filings, got 105 (exactly half).

proposals.title: C15 freq +1: Q6 iter_2 missing GROUP BY key — exact 50% factor mismatch
proposals.detect: Q6 iter_2: rows BUENAVENTURA MINING CO INC and SOUTHERN CO each had value and count exactly 50% of expected. Classic C15 signature confirmed by N× factor diagnostic (C30).
proposals.fix: Audit all group-by hash map key structs against the SQL GROUP BY clause. C15 now has a precise diagnostic: if value AND count are both off by the SAME integer factor N simultaneously, a GROUP BY key dimension is missing.

proposals.title: P17 freq +1: Q6 iter_1 catastrophic 46.8s aggregation_merge — shared map merge
proposals.detect: Q6 iter_1: aggregation_merge = 46,800ms with 64 threads. Root cause: each thread's local unordered_map was merged sequentially into a shared unordered_map (O(N × lookup) with lock contention). 1.73M pre-table rows × 64 threads = catastrophic.
proposals.fix: NEVER merge thread-local aggregation maps sequentially into a shared map. Always use P17/P20 pattern: occupied-slot tracking + parallel sort-merge, or direct atomic CAS on a shared open-addressing table. Thread-local merge must be the DEFAULT (P20).

proposals.title: P20 freq +1 + P17 freq +1: Q24 iter_3 agg_merge 167ms → 1ms with thread-local aggregation
proposals.detect: Q24 iter_3: switching from shared aggregation map to thread-local maps with occupied-slot tracking reduced aggregation_merge from 167ms to 1ms. Classic P17/P20 win replicated.
proposals.fix: Thread-local aggregation is ALWAYS the default pattern for parallel GROUP BY. Apply at code-generation time, not as a post-hoc optimization.

proposals.title: NEW P25: Large thread-local agg merge with parallel sort-merge still slow at 64 threads — use two-phase tree-merge
proposals.detect: Q6 iter_5: aggregation_merge still 385ms even with occupied-slot tracking + __gnu_parallel::sort. AGG_CAP=2M per thread × 64 threads → ~2M total occupied entries × 32 bytes = 64MB to sort and reduce. Phase dominates total runtime (38%).
proposals.fix: Two-phase tree merge: (1) merge 8 thread-local tables into one intermediate (8 parallel reduce tasks, each sequential), producing nthreads/8 intermediate tables; (2) sequential merge of 8 intermediates. This reduces sort input by 8× and keeps data in L3. Alternatively: reduce AGG_CAP to next_pow2(actual_group_cardinality × 2) (apply P23 logic to aggregation, not just joins) — smaller tables = less to collect and sort.

proposals.title: NEW: storage_design.json C29 WARNING annotation must be treated as a HARD CONSTRAINT
proposals.detect: Q3: storage_design.json value column note explicitly says 'C29 WARNING: SUM must accumulate as int64_t cents: iv=llround(v*100); sum+=iv; output=sum/100.' Code generator read the file but used Kahan instead. The annotation was ignored.
proposals.fix: Code inspector pre-flight: grep storage_design.json for 'C29 WARNING'. If found for any column used in SUM/AVG, reject any code that uses double/long double accumulation for that column. Only int64_t cents accumulation is acceptable. Kahan, Neumaier, and long double are all WRONG fixes for C29.

meta.notes: Q3 is the critical failure — storage_design.json explicitly annotated the fix but code generator applied incorrect Kahan alternative. Q6 passed correctness at iter_3 but remains SLOW due to parallel sort-merge aggregation bottleneck at 64-thread scale.
